title: cryptogramma acrostichoides subsp. cascadensis
intro: Cryptogramma acrostichoides subsp. cascadensis, also known as the rock fern or Cascade goldenback fern, is a subspecies of the Cryptogramma acrostichoides plant, a type of fern native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Cryptogramma acrostichoides subsp. cascadensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and it is often used topically to treat wounds and skin conditions.
